title: Microsoft Access Database
description: Schema for a Microsoft Access Database object as defined in the DAO (Data Access Objects) reference. A Database object represents an open Access database (.accdb or .mdb) and provides methods and properties to manipulate its structure and data, including tables, queries, relations, and containers.

name.description: The name and path of the database file. For a Microsoft Access database, this is the full file path to the .accdb or .mdb file.

version.description: The version of the Microsoft Access database engine that created the database. Returns a four-character string representing the engine version number.

collatingOrder.description: A value that specifies the sequence of the sort order in text for string comparison or sorting. Corresponds to locale-specific collation constants such as dbLangGeneral.

connect.description: A value that provides information about the source of an open database, a database used in a pass-through query, or a linked table. Contains the connection string for ODBC or external data sources.

queryTimeout.description: A value that specifies the number of seconds to wait before a timeout error occurs when a query is executed on an ODBC data source.

recordsAffected.description: The number of records affected by the most recently invoked Execute method. Read-only.

updatable.description: A value that indicates whether changes can be made to the database. True if the database can be modified; False if it is read-only. Read-only.

transactions.description: A value that indicates whether the database supports transactions (BeginTrans, CommitTrans, and Rollback). Read-only.

designMasterID.description: A 16-byte value that uniquely identifies the Design Master in a replica set. Used in database replication scenarios.

replicaID.description: A 16-byte value that uniquely identifies a database replica. Read-only.

tableDefs.description: A collection of all stored TableDef objects in the database. Each TableDef represents the definition of a base table or linked table.

queryDefs.description: A collection of all stored QueryDef objects in the database. Each QueryDef represents the definition of a saved query.

relations.description: A collection of all stored Relation objects in the database. Each Relation defines a relationship between fields in tables.

containers.description: A collection of all Container objects in the database. Containers store information about database objects such as forms, reports, tables, and modules for security purposes.

TableDefSummary.description: A summary representation of a TableDef object within the Database's TableDefs collection.

attributes.description: A value that indicates one or more characteristics of the TableDef object. Combines constants such as dbAttachExclusive, dbAttachSavePWD, dbSystemObject, dbHiddenObject, and dbAttachedTable.

dateCreated.description: The date and time the table was created. Read-only.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time of the most recent change made to the table. Read-only.

recordCount.description: The number of records in the table. For linked tables, this value is always -1.

sourceTableName.description: For linked tables, the name of the source table in the external data source. Empty for base tables.

connect.description: For linked tables, the connection string to the external data source. Empty for base tables.

QueryDef.description: A QueryDef object represents a saved query definition in the database. Contains the SQL statement and metadata for the query.

type.description: A value that indicates the type of query, such as select (0), crosstab (16), delete (32), update (48), append (64), make-table (80), DDL (96), SQL pass-through (112), union (128), or bulk (144).

sql.description: The SQL statement that defines the query.

dateCreated.description: The date and time the query was created. Read-only.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time of the most recent change to the query. Read-only.

updatable.description: Whether the query definition can be changed.

connect.description: The connection string for a pass-through query.

returnsRecords.description: For SQL pass-through queries, whether the query returns records.

Relation.description: A Relation object represents a relationship between fields in tables or queries. Used to enforce referential integrity between a primary table and a foreign table.

table.description: The name of the primary (referenced) table in the relationship.

foreignTable.description: The name of the foreign (referencing) table in the relationship.

attributes.description: A value that indicates the relationship type and enforcement rules. Combines constants: dbRelationUnique (1), dbRelationDontEnforce (2), dbRelationInherited (4), dbRelationUpdateCascade (256), dbRelationDeleteCascade (4096), dbRelationLeft (16777216), dbRelationRight (33554432).

fields.description: The fields involved in the relationship, mapping primary key fields to foreign key fields.

name.description: The name of the field in the primary table.

foreignName.description: The name of the corresponding field in the foreign table.

Container.description: A Container object groups similar types of Document objects for security and access control. Built-in containers include Databases, Tables, and Relations.

lastUpdated.description: When the document was last modified.
